title: Urban Steps Cinematic Portrait
description: Contemplative cinematic portrait sitting on outdoor stone steps in front of a glowing building entrance.
prompt: A cinematic urban portrait of me, keeping my real face unchanged. I am sitting casually on outdoor stone steps in front of a building entrance, leaning slightly forward with a confident and contemplative posture. My left elbow rests on my knee, with my hand raised to my temple in a thoughtful gesture, while my right arm hangs more loosely, with my hand extended downward in a relaxed position. My legs are bent naturally, spreading apart for a grounded and strong presence. My gaze is directed toward the camera, steady and intense, with a calm yet powerful expression. I am wearing a black outfit: a fitted turtleneck sweater layered under a black coat with a wide collar and subtle texture. The coat has a tailored yet modern look, with a slightly matte fabric that absorbs the light, creating depth. My trousers are also black, slim-fitted, completing the clean, monochromatic style. No visible accessories, emphasizing minimalism and sophistication. The background shows part of an urban building with glass doors and warm interior lights softly glowing, adding contrast to the darker tones of my outfit. The lighting is warm and diffused, highlighting my face and upper body while creating soft shadows that add cinematic depth. The camera captures me slightly from below (low angle), emphasizing strength and presence, framed from the knees up. The focal length resembles a portrait lens around 50-85mm, producing natural proportions with a shallow depth of field that keeps me sharp against the softly blurred background. Style: cinematic, moody urban portrait, editorial fashion photography, minimalistic monochrome outfit, professional model vibe. Keep the face remain unchanged, strictly. Strictly preserve the original face identity. The face must remain 100% unchanged across all generations, with no changes to facial features, structure, expression, skin tone, age, or identity.

title: Professional Email Writer
description: Write any professional email in seconds with perfect tone and structure.
prompt:
You are an expert professional email writer.

Write a clear, polished, and action-oriented email based on the details I provide.

Before writing, understand:

* Who the email is being sent to
* The purpose of the email
* The desired tone
* The level of urgency
* The outcome I want from the recipient

Email details:

* Recipient:
* Sender/context:
* Purpose of the email:
* Key points to include:
* Desired tone: professional / warm / direct / persuasive / polite / firm
* Urgency level: low / medium / high
* Desired action from recipient:
* Any deadline:
* Any words or phrases to avoid:

Output format:

1. Subject line
2. Final email body
3. Short alternate subject lines
4. Optional shorter version of the email

Writing rules:

* Keep the email professional and easy to read.
* Start with a natural opening.
* Avoid robotic or overly formal language.
* Make the message concise but complete.
* Use simple business English.
* Add a clear call-to-action.
* Avoid unnecessary repetition.
* If the topic is sensitive, keep the tone polite and solution-focused.
* If information is missing, make reasonable assumptions and keep the email adaptable.

Now write the final email.

title: SEO Blog Post Generator
description: Generate a complete, SEO-optimized blog post on any topic.
prompt:
Act as an expert content writer and SEO specialist. Write a comprehensive blog post on: [topic]

Target keyword: [primary keyword]
Secondary keywords: [list 3-5 related keywords]
Target audience: [describe your reader]
Word count: [desired length]
Tone: [informative/conversational/authoritative]

Structure the post with:
1. Attention-grabbing H1 title (include primary keyword)
2. Compelling introduction with a hook
3. Well-organized body with H2 and H3 subheadings
4. Actionable tips or insights in each section
5. Real examples or statistics where relevant
6. Strong conclusion with a clear takeaway
7. SEO meta description (150-160 characters)

Ensure natural keyword placement, short paragraphs for readability, and a writing style that keeps readers engaged from start to finish.

title: AI Prompt Optimizer
description: Transform weak prompts into powerful, precise instructions that get perfect results.
prompt:
You are an expert AI prompt engineer.

Your task is to improve my rough prompt into a clear, powerful, and high-performing prompt that gives better results from AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Midjourney, Veo, or other AI systems.

My rough prompt:
[Paste rough prompt here]

First, analyze the prompt and identify:

* What the prompt is trying to achieve
* What information is missing
* Where the prompt is vague
* What output format should be requested
* What constraints should be added
* What examples or context would improve the result

Then rewrite the prompt using this structure:

1. Role
2. Goal
3. Context
4. Inputs
5. Exact task
6. Output format
7. Constraints
8. Quality rules
9. Edge cases
10. Final instruction

Output format:
A. Improved prompt
B. Why this version is better
C. Optional shorter version
D. Optional advanced version

Rules:

* Do not make the prompt unnecessarily long.
* Keep it practical and usable.
* Preserve the original intent.
* Add clarity, structure, and specificity.
* Add exact output format where useful.
* Add examples only if they improve quality.
* Avoid generic wording.
* Make the final prompt copy-ready.

Now optimize my prompt.

title: Academic Essay Writer
description: Write well-structured, research-backed academic essays on any subject.
prompt:
You are a distinguished academic writer with expertise across multiple disciplines. Write a comprehensive essay on:

Topic: [your essay topic]
Academic level: [high school/undergraduate/graduate/PhD]
Word count: [required length]
Citation style: [APA/MLA/Chicago/Harvard]
Subject area: [discipline or course]
Argument or thesis direction: [the position you want to argue, or leave blank for balanced analysis]

Essay structure:
1. Introduction with a compelling hook and clear thesis statement
2. Literature review or background context
3. Well-developed body paragraphs with topic sentences, evidence, and analysis
4. Counterargument and rebuttal section
5. Synthesis and discussion of implications
6. Conclusion that reinforces thesis and provides forward-looking perspective
7. References list formatted correctly

Ensure academic language, logical flow, critical thinking, and proper citation integration throughout.

title: Email Marketing Campaign
description: Write a complete email marketing campaign sequence that converts subscribers.
prompt:
You are an expert email marketing strategist and copywriter.

Create a complete email marketing campaign for the product, offer, or brand I describe.

Campaign details:

* Brand/product:
* Target audience:
* Campaign goal: sales / signups / reactivation / launch / awareness / retention
* Offer or hook:
* Customer pain point:
* Key benefits:
* Tone: premium / friendly / urgent / educational / luxury / playful / professional
* Number of emails required:
* Campaign duration:
* CTA:
* Any discount or deadline:
* Words or claims to avoid:

Create a complete campaign with:

1. Campaign strategy summary
2. Audience angle
3. Email sequence plan
4. Subject lines for each email
5. Preview text for each email
6. Full email copy for each email
7. CTA for each email
8. Recommended send timing
9. A/B test ideas
10. Final conversion tips

Writing rules:

* Make every email feel different.
* Do not repeat the same message.
* Use strong but believable copy.
* Focus on benefits, not only features.
* Keep subject lines short and clickable.
* Avoid spammy words unless necessary.
* Add urgency naturally if relevant.
* Make the campaign feel human and brand-safe.
* Use simple language and clear CTAs.

Now create the email marketing campaign.

title: Brand Voice and Tone Guide
description: Define your brand's unique voice, tone, and messaging framework.
prompt:
You are a senior brand strategist. Develop a comprehensive brand voice and tone guide for:

Brand name: [company name]
Industry: [market sector]
Mission: [what you stand for]
Core values: [list 3-5 values]
Target audience: [who you serve]
Brand personality words: [3-5 adjectives]

Deliver: brand personality archetype with rationale, voice characteristics, tone variations for different contexts, vocabulary guide (words we use vs avoid), writing principles with before/after examples, dos and don'ts, sample copy across 5 formats, and guidance for external writers.

title: ATS Resume Optimizer
description: Optimize your resume to pass Applicant Tracking Systems and get more interviews.
prompt:
You are an expert resume writer and ATS optimization specialist.

Optimize my resume for the job description I provide.

Inputs:
Resume:
[Paste resume here]

Job description:
[Paste job description here]

Your task:

1. Analyze the job description.
2. Identify the most important keywords, skills, tools, responsibilities, and success metrics.
3. Compare my resume against the job description.
4. Find missing keywords and weak sections.
5. Rewrite my resume content to improve ATS match and recruiter readability.

Output format:
A. ATS match analysis
B. Missing keywords
C. Resume improvement recommendations
D. Improved professional summary
E. Improved work experience bullet points
F. Skills section rewrite
G. Final ATS-friendly resume version
H. Additional suggestions for LinkedIn profile

Resume writing rules:

* Keep the resume honest and based only on my real experience.
* Do not invent fake achievements.
* Strengthen weak bullet points using action verbs.
* Add measurable impact where possible.
* Use keywords naturally.
* Keep language clear and professional.
* Avoid overused phrases like “hardworking” or “team player”.
* Make the resume suitable for both ATS and human recruiters.
* Use concise bullet points.
* Prioritize impact, ownership, tools, and outcomes.

If some metrics are missing, suggest placeholders I can fill in.

Now optimize my resume for this job.

title: LinkedIn Profile Optimizer
description: Transform your LinkedIn profile into a magnet for recruiters and opportunities.
prompt:
You are a LinkedIn optimization expert. Completely optimize my LinkedIn profile:

Current headline: [your current headline]
Current about section: [paste current about]
Current experience descriptions: [paste 2-3 recent roles]
Industry: [your field]
Career goal: [what roles or opportunities you want]
Top 5 skills: [your strongest skills]
Key achievement: [your proudest professional accomplishment]

Rewrite the headline (220 char max), about section (2600 char max storytelling structure), experience descriptions with quantified results, top 50 skills to add, featured section ideas, and engagement tips for algorithm visibility.

title: User Research Interview Guide
description: Design a comprehensive user research interview to uncover real user needs.
prompt:
You are a senior UX researcher.

Create a complete user research interview guide for the product, feature, or problem I describe.

Research context:

* Product/feature:
* Target users:
* Research goal:
* Business goal:
* What we already know:
* What we need to learn:
* Interview duration:
* User segment:
* Market/context:
* Any assumptions to validate:

Create a detailed interview guide with:

1. Research objective
2. Participant criteria
3. Moderator introduction script
4. Warm-up questions
5. Core discovery questions
6. Behavior-based questions
7. Pain point questions
8. Motivation and decision-making questions
9. Feature-specific questions
10. Pricing or willingness-to-pay questions if relevant
11. Closing questions
12. Follow-up probes
13. What not to ask
14. Observation checklist
15. Synthesis framework

Question rules:

* Avoid leading questions.
* Ask about past behavior, not only opinions.
* Use open-ended questions.
* Include “why” and “tell me more” probes.
* Keep the tone neutral and friendly.
* Separate user needs from solution assumptions.
* Focus on real workflows, frustrations, and decision points.

Also provide:

* A short 30-minute version
* A deeper 60-minute version
* Key insights template after the interview

Now create the user research interview guide.

title: Wireframe and Prototype Brief
description: Create a detailed wireframe brief that aligns design and development teams.
prompt:
You are a senior UX designer. Write a comprehensive wireframe brief for:

Product: [app/website/feature]
Platform: [web/iOS/Android/responsive]
User story: [as a [user], I want to [action] so that [benefit]]
Scope: [which screens or flows]
Business objective: [what metric this improves]

Provide a flow diagram, screen-by-screen breakdown (purpose, elements, content hierarchy, interactive behavior, states), navigation patterns, a component inventory, accessibility notes, edge cases, and design decision rationale.

title: Expert Code Debugger
description: Diagnose and fix any coding bug with systematic debugging methodology.
prompt:
You are an expert software engineer and debugging assistant.

Help me debug the issue I am facing in my codebase.

Problem details:

* Tech stack:
* Framework/library:
* Error message:
* Expected behavior:
* Actual behavior:
* Relevant code:
* Recent changes made:
* Environment: local / staging / production
* Browser/device if applicable:
* Steps to reproduce:

Your task:

1. Understand the bug.
2. Identify the most likely root causes.
3. Explain the issue in simple terms.
4. Suggest a step-by-step debugging plan.
5. Provide the corrected code where possible.
6. Mention any risky assumptions.
7. Suggest tests to confirm the fix.
8. Suggest preventive improvements.

Output format:
A. Quick diagnosis
B. Most likely root cause
C. Step-by-step fix
D. Corrected code
E. Why the fix works
F. Test cases
G. Prevention checklist

Debugging rules:

* Do not guess blindly.
* If information is missing, ask for the exact missing detail.
* Prefer minimal safe changes first.
* Do not rewrite the whole app unless required.
* Preserve existing architecture.
* Explain tradeoffs.
* Highlight if the issue is frontend, backend, config, dependency, data, routing, state, or deployment related.
* If multiple fixes are possible, recommend the safest one.

Now debug my issue.

title: REST API Design and Documentation
description: Design a clean, well-documented REST API following industry best practices.
prompt:
You are a backend architect who designs APIs used by millions of developers. Design a complete REST API for:

Application: [describe your application]
Core resources: [main entities, e.g. users, products, orders]
Authentication method: [JWT/OAuth2/API key]
Database: [PostgreSQL/MongoDB/MySQL/etc]
Language/framework: [Node.js Express/Python FastAPI/Go/etc]

Design the resource structure, endpoint documentation per resource (method, URL, request/response schema, status codes, rate limiting), authentication flow, error response standard, pagination design, filtering/sorting conventions, an OpenAPI 3.0 spec outline, and security considerations.

title: Investor Pitch Deck Creator
description: Build a compelling investor pitch deck that gets meetings with top VCs.
prompt:
You are an expert startup pitch deck strategist.

Create a compelling investor pitch deck for my startup idea.

Startup details:

* Startup name:
* One-line idea:
* Target customer:
* Problem:
* Current solution alternatives:
* Our solution:
* Product status: idea / MVP / live / revenue
* Market:
* Business model:
* Traction:
* Competitors:
* Differentiation:
* Team:
* Funding ask:
* Use of funds:
* Key metrics:
* Vision:

Create a complete investor pitch deck structure with:

1. Title slide
2. Problem
3. Why now
4. Target customer
5. Solution
6. Product demo/story
7. Market opportunity
8. Business model
9. Traction
10. Go-to-market strategy
11. Competition
12. Differentiation/moat
13. Financial model overview
14. Team
15. Funding ask
16. Use of funds
17. Closing vision slide

For each slide, provide:

* Slide title
* Main message
* Bullet points
* Suggested visual
* Speaker notes
* What investors should understand from the slide

Writing rules:

* Make it clear, sharp, and investor-friendly.
* Avoid vague startup buzzwords.
* Focus on problem, market, traction, and business model.
* Highlight why this can become large.
* Keep each slide concise.
* Use strong storytelling.
* Include metrics wherever possible.
* If traction is weak, position MVP, customer validation, or early signals clearly.
* Make the pitch realistic and credible.

Also provide:
A. 30-second elevator pitch
B. 2-minute verbal pitch
C. Common investor questions and best answers
D. Weaknesses in the current pitch and how to improve them

Now create the pitch deck content.

title: Standard Operating Procedure Writer
description: Create clear, comprehensive SOPs that any team member can follow.
prompt:
You are an operations specialist who has built SOPs for scaling companies. Write a complete Standard Operating Procedure for:

Process name: [name of the process]
Department: [which team owns this]
Frequency: [how often this is done]
Trigger: [what starts this process]
People involved: [roles that participate]
Systems used: [tools, software, platforms]

Create the header section, process overview, a RACI roles matrix, numbered step-by-step instructions with decision points and time estimates, quality checks, a troubleshooting guide, and a glossary.
